# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Cache Dependencies and Build Outputs

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop
      - 'feature/**'
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Cache Node.js dependencies
      - name: Cache Node.js modules
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.npm # Caches the global npm cache directory
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json', '**/yarn.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-node-

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20' # Or your desired Node.js version

      - name: Install Node.js dependencies
        run: npm ci # Use npm ci for clean installs in CI

      # Cache Python dependencies
      - name: Cache Python packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip # Caches the pip cache directory
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt', '**/pyproject.toml', '**/setup.py') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.x' # Or your desired Python version
          cache: 'pip' # This step also has built-in pip cache support, but a dedicated step gives more control.

      - name: Install Python dependencies
        run: pip install -r requirements.txt # Or your preferred installation command (e.g., pip install .)

      # Cache project configuration files, environment-specific configs, and build outputs
      # This cache is specific to the current branch to accommodate environment-specific configs.
      - name: Cache project configs and build outputs
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            .config
            config
            environments
            dist # Example build output directory
            build # Another example build output directory
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-project-${{ github.ref }}-${{ hashFiles('**/.config/**', '**/*config*.yml', '**/*config*.json', '**/.env*', 'environments/**', 'package.json', 'pyproject.toml') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-project-${{ github.ref }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-project- # Fallback to a general cache for the OS

      # Example steps for building and testing your application
      - name: Run Node.js build
        run: npm run build # Or your Node.js build command

      - name: Run Node.js tests
        run: npm test # Or your Node.js test command

      - name: Run Python tests
        run: pytest # Or your Python test command

      # Optional: Upload build artifacts if needed for subsequent jobs or download
      # - name: Upload build artifact
      #   uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      #   with:
      #     name: my-app-build
      #     path: dist/ # Or your build output directory
